name: Embalse de Cubillas — circular desde Atarfe — Sierra Elvira
region_intro: Hiking around Atarfe offers diverse landscapes, from the Sierra Elvira mountain range to the fertile Vega de Granada. The region features varied terrain, including gentle paths through olive groves and more challenging mountain ascents. Hikers can explore geological formations and enjoy panoramic views of Granada and the Sierra Nevada mountains. This area provides a unique natural environment surrounded by mountains and agricultural land. Best hiking trails around Atarfe The most popular hiking route is Peaks of Sierra Elvira Trail (PR-A 445), a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 50 minutes to complete. This challenging route offers extensive views from the summits of Sierra Elvira. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ermita de los Tres Juanes Trail, a moderate 2.6 miles (4.1 km) path. This trail leads to a historic hermitage with panoramic views of Granada and the Sierra Nevada. Local hikers also love the Caparacena Trail (SL-A 305), a 4.1 miles (6.7 km) trail leading through olive groves and rolling hills, often completed in about 2 hours. Hiking around Atarfe is defined by the Sierra Elvira mountain range, fertile plains of the Vega de Granada, and extensive olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to difficult ascents. The routes in Atarfe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Atarfe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Atarfe?
A: Atarfe offers a good selection of hiking trails, with nearly 20 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy walks through olive groves to more challenging mountain ascents in the Sierra Elvira.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on hikes around Atarfe?
A: The terrain around Atarfe is quite diverse. You'll find gentle paths winding through the fertile Vega de Granada, characterized by poplar trees and irrigated plains. In contrast, the Sierra Elvira mountain range offers varied geological formations, from rolling hills to more rugged ascents, providing panoramic views.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Atarfe?
A: Yes, Atarfe has trails suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351408778/caparacena-trail-sl-a-305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caparacena Trail (SL-A 305)&lt;/a&gt; is an easier 6.7 km hike that winds through olive groves and offers views of rolling hills, typically completed in about 2 hours.

Q: What are some challenging hikes in the Atarfe area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985022096/peaks-of-sierra-elvira-trail-pr-a-445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peaks of Sierra Elvira Trail (PR-A 445)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.4 km route offering extensive views from the summits. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351351261/sierra-elvira-integral-trail-morron-de-la-punta-de-enmedio-and-el-piorno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Elvira Integral Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 7.2 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any hikes with good viewpoints or landmarks?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in the Sierra Elvira offer stunning panoramic views of Granada and the majestic Sierra Nevada mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351388942/ermita-de-los-tres-juanes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de los Tres Juanes Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads to a historic hermitage on Cerro del Castillejo, providing excellent vistas. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351451949/sierra-elvira-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Elvira Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers great views.

Q: What can I see or do near the hiking trails in Atarfe?
A: Beyond the trails, Atarfe and its surroundings offer cultural and natural attractions. You can explore historical sites like the remains of Madinat Ilbira or visit the Iglesia Parroquial de la Encarnación. For natural beauty, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5511812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; or the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/394966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lower Gardens of the Generalife&lt;/a&gt;, both within easy reach.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Atarfe?
A: The hiking routes in Atarfe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the stunning panoramic views of Granada and the Sierra Nevada, and the variety of trails suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Atarfe?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Atarfe are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. This includes popular options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985022096/peaks-of-sierra-elvira-trail-pr-a-445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peaks of Sierra Elvira Trail (PR-A 445)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351388942/ermita-de-los-tres-juanes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de los Tres Juanes Trail&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient exploration of the area.

Q: Is Atarfe suitable for family-friendly hikes?
A: Atarfe offers several options for families. The region has trails described as easy to moderate, with lengths typically ranging from 5 to 10 km, making them manageable for children. The varied scenery, from olive groves to mountain views, can keep younger hikers engaged.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Atarfe?
A: The best time to hike in Atarfe is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, especially in the open plains, while winter offers a different experience, though some higher trails might be cooler.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails in Atarfe?
A: Atarfe itself is a municipality with various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. The Los Pinos de la Moleona recreational area, which connects several trails in Sierra Elvira, also offers amenities like picnic tables, making it a convenient spot for a break.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Atarfe?
A: Many trailheads in Atarfe, particularly those leading into the Sierra Elvira, offer parking. The Los Pinos de la Moleona recreational area is known for providing ample parking, serving as a convenient starting point for several adventures. Baños de Sierra Elvira is another district that serves as a good starting point with parking access.
tips: The Sierra de la Elvira is a mountainous massif located in the region of La Vega, in the province of Granada. With a maximum altitude of 1,118 meters, it offers spectacular views of the city of Granada and its surroundings. In addition, it has a great natural wealth, with numerous animal and plant species, as well as archaeological remains that testify to its historical importance.
Large esplanade where you can take a breather and enjoy the fantastic views of the swamp. It is perfectly accessible by bicycle.

Many recreational activities such as windsurfing, canoeing and jet skiing are permitted on this lake. A large picnic area has been created on its north shore, once past the dam. In this area you will also find a restaurant. This body of water allows you to take very beautiful photos, especially on a windless day when the Sierra Nevada is covered with snow.
The Cubillas Reservoir is one of the main bird watching areas in the province of Granada, as it serves as a transit station for migrations and a nesting space for some species.
